*Narfa'u darajatin man nash'a'u innaka rabbika hakeemun 'aleem*. *Darajat* is plural, and it also has *tanween*, which is why the correct translation will be: We raise degrees upon degrees to whom We will. Above, under *Wa liyakoona minal-mooqineen*, we have pointed toward the truth that this is the expression, in other words, that those who reflect upon the Divine Kingdom, Allah similarly raises their degrees in knowledge, gnosis, faith, and certainty. The reference to the attributes of *Hakeem* (Wise) and *'Aleem* (Knowing) here points toward the same *Sunnatullah* which Allah has fixed for this wishing of His. That is, His wishing is based on His knowledge and wisdom. He does not distribute the degrees of His proximity blindly; rather, He grants them to those who are worthy of it and who fulfill His rights. Within it, there is a highly subtle indication that if a person does not use his intellect and thought, he makes stones fashioned by his own hands gods and bows before them, hoping for their fulfillment of needs; and if he uses his intellect and thought, then the sun and moon, Venus and Jupiter, all become dust in his path.

**The Honor of Being Remembered (*Raf'-e-Thikr*) for Prophet Abraham in This World:** 
Above, in the verses, the account of Prophet Abraham's faith and gnosis was stated alongside his call. Now, it is being told that in this world, Allah granted him and his call the honor of *raf'-e-thikr* (exalted remembrance) and eternal fame. Among his descendants were great prophets and high-ranking righteous people and reformers, and the religion of all of them was the same religion of monotheism which Prophet Abraham invited to, and Noah before him. The meaning is that this is the shared religion of all prophets, and you too are inviting to this same religion. If your nation does not wish to accept it, then do not care for it; God has raised others who will become its bearers. You have to follow the religion of those to whom Allah's guidance was granted, despite their opposition. So follow them, and tell these opponents clearly: I am not a seeker of any reward from you so that if you did not buy goods from my shop, my shop would close down. Whatever I am presenting before you is merely a reminder. Accept it, and it is your own benefit; if you do not accept it, you will suffer yourself, nothing will be lost from me.

*Wa wahabna lahu Ishaqa wa Ya'quba...* (And We gave him Isaac and Jacob...). Isaac is the son of Prophet Abraham, and Jacob (peace be upon him) is his grandson. It is stated: *Kullan hadayna* (All of them We guided)—We guided each of them to the same religion of monotheism and the same straight path which Prophet Abraham invited to. *Wa Noohan hadayna min qabl* (And Noah, We guided before). This also points to the path of guidance from before Abraham, that Noah was also guided by Us to the same path, and he invited to it. It is known from the Talmud that Prophet Noah also trained Prophet Abraham. From this aspect, as if the connections from above and below were joined.

*Wa min thurriyyatihi* (And from his descendants). The pronoun returns to Prophet Abraham. It is stated: among his descendants, We also blessed David, Solomon, Job, Joseph, Moses, and Aaron with the guidance of the same straight path. Here, this matter... 

**Prophets Who Were Also Granted Political Power Alongside Prophethood:** 
Here, keep it in view that all these honorable figures are from among the Israelite prophets, and along with this, this characteristic is shared among them that each of them attained kingship or some form of political leadership. *Kathalika najzil-muhsinin* (Thus do We reward the doers of good) points to the fact that they attained this guidance because they were doers of good; Allah granted them the abilities, which they used correctly, so Allah blessed them with His favors. As if this has no connection with ancestral inheritance, but entirely with qualities and morals.

*Wa Zakariyya wa Yahya...* (And Zechariah and John...). Zechariah, John, and Jesus (peace be upon them) are among the famous prophets. Elias refers to the Elijah of the Torah. Regarding these prophets too, it is stated that Allah guided them to the same straight path to which He guided Abraham and other prophets. They were also callers to monotheism and Islam, not to any other religion. *Kullun minas-saliheen* (all were among the righteous). From this, the same thing was derived which was indicated above—that whatever they attained, they attained on the basis of their righteousness and piety; none of them held the status of divinity as the Christians presumed regarding Prophet Jesus (peace be upon him). Just as kingship and leadership hold the status of a shared attribute among the aforementioned prophets, similarly, the glory of asceticism, poverty (*faqr*), and detachment is a shared attribute among these prophets.

*Wa Isma'eela wal-Yasa'a wa Yunusa wa Lutan...* (And Ishmael and Elisha and Jonah and Lot...). 
**Investigation into Elisha:** 
Among these names, three names—Ishmael, Jonah, and Lot—are famous; the accounts of these prophets are also narrated in the Quran. However, there are two prophets whose names are similar to Elisha. One is Elisha, whose era is told as 13th century BC, the other is Isaiah, whose era is told as 620 BC. The first name is closer to the Quran's pronunciation. *Wa kullan faddalna 'alal-'alameen* (And all [of them] We favored over the worlds) points to that specific excellence which every prophet attains due to his office. Since a prophet is appointed for the guidance of the creation, he attains this excellence. Here, the qualities stated for these prophets are the shared qualities of all prophets. Mentioning them separately is not intended for specification, but merely for enumeration, so that the reader's attention may be drawn toward each quality separately.

*Wa min aba'ihim wa thurriyyatihim...* (And among their fathers and their descendants...). That is, this guidance did not remain limited to these prophets alone; among their fathers, their descendants, and their brethren as well, many attained this blessing. It is stated that We showed them the path, chose them, and guided them to the straight path. *Ijti-ba'* here refers to that chosenness which Allah granted them for the invitation and propagation of His monotheism and guidance. *Sirat-e-Mustaqeem* (Straight Path) refers here to the path of monotheism, and its indefinite form here is for the sake of the magnificence of status.

**Guidance is That Which Was Obtained by the Prophets:** 
*Thalika hudallahu* (That is the guidance of Allah). That is, this guidance which was obtained by all these prophets and their followers is the only guidance of Allah; all paths besides this are those invented by Satan. This path Allah opens for those of His servants for whom He wills. *"For whom He wills"* points to that *Sunnatullah* which He has fixed for faith and guidance. We have clarified its detail in more than one place. It is stated: these people too, whom Allah granted these ranks, if they had become involved in polytheism, all their deeds would have collapsed. Their chosenness would not have remained on the sole basis that they are the descendants of Noah or Abraham. This warning is also for the Arabs and for the Children of Israel—that those who, by deviating from monotheism, are dreaming of chosenness merely on the basis of the relationship they attained due to being the descendants of Abraham, are involved in sheer foolishness. This, let alone—even if they too had become polluted with polytheism, no weight would have remained for them before God.

*Ula'ikallatheena ataynahumul-kitaba wal-hukma wan-nubuwwah...* (Those are the ones to whom We gave the Scripture and authority and prophethood...). Alongside the Book, the word *hikmah* (wisdom) has often come in the Quran. Here, the word *hukm* (authority/judgment) is used. Master Imam, in his book *Mufradat al-Quran*, while discussing the word *hukm*, writes that *hukm* refers to correct understanding and deciding matters in the light of correct understanding. This very thing, when it becomes firm and takes the status of a deep-rooted faculty, is called *hikmah* (wisdom). *Hukm* and *hikmah* are among the concomitants of the Divine Book, because the true purpose of the Divine Book is to give guidance in the affairs of life, whether that life is individual or collective, and whether the situation to be faced has been explicitly stated in the Book or its ruling has to be extracted through *ijtihad* (independent reasoning) and *istinbat* (deduction). Like the Book, this *hukm* is also a divine gift, and it is through this that the Divine Book becomes a practical reality of life. If the bearers of the Book become deprived of this, the Book becomes indifferent to the affairs and issues of life and remains nothing more than a historical document kept in some museum of curiosities.

Like the Book, this *hukm* is also a divine gift, and it is through this that the Divine Book becomes a practical reality of life. If the bearers of the Book become deprived of this, the Book becomes indifferent to the affairs and issues of life and remains nothing more than a historical document kept in some museum of curiosities.

The meaning of the verse is: these people mentioned above, whom We honored with the blessing of the Book, *hukm*, and prophethood, and they appreciated this blessing—now, with these very blessings, We have wished to grace these people (pointing toward the people of Mecca) through your medium (pointing toward the Prophet, peace and blessings of Allah be upon him). The truth was that they should have appreciated this blessing, but if these people are ungrateful to this blessing, you do not care for them; We have appointed for the fulfillment of its responsibility those who are not going to deny it. In *wakkalna biha*, along with being made responsible, the concept of being a guarantor and trustee is also present; the letter *ba* (*bi*) is evidence of this. *Qawman* refers to the group of Companions who had entered Islam by then, and also those for whom it was decreed to enter its bearers afterward, even if at the time of the revelation of these verses, they had not entered them. In this segment, there is a prophecy of the steadfastness of the Companions and the abundance of the Ummah in the future, and from this aspect, this is a great glad tiding for the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) which was given to him in that era when he was passing through a storm of oppositions. The words express the decisive decree of Allah which has been made for the success of this call, and an indication is also emerging that do not look at the small number of those whom Allah has raised for this; these very people will be the standard-bearers of this call, and these very drops will one day become an ocean.

*Ula'ikallatheena hadallahu...* (Those are the ones whom Allah has guided...). *Iqtadih* has the letter *ha* for pause and silence. It is stated: these are the people whom Allah honored with His guidance, so you follow the footsteps of these very people and follow the guidance they obtained. As for these people who are claimants to being the heirs of Abraham but are following Satan and are not ready to listen to your words, then leave them to their state; tell them that I am not seeking any reward from you for this so that if you did not buy goods from my shop, my shop would sit down. I have received this from my Lord for free, and I am distributing it for free. If you accept it, there is your own benefit; if you reject it, you will remain deprived yourself. This is the invitation of the religion of God; this is not any trade or shop-keeping that if you do not become customers, my business would sit down. My reward is with Allah. *In huwa illa thikra lil-'alameen* (It is not but a reminder for the worlds). In this, the referent of *huwa* is the Quran, and this is further clarification of the same subject. The meaning is: this Quran is merely a reminder and a reminder for people. If the Prophet has conveyed this reminder, he has absolved himself of his responsibility. If people do not accept it, they will see its end themselves. By the word *thikra* (reminder) for the Quran, two truths are indicated. One is that whatever it is presenting is no strange or unique thing; rather, it is a reminder of those same truths that are deposited within human nature, but people have buried them under their desires and innovations. Second, it is a reminder of that same divine guidance that all prophets from Noah to Abraham brought, but the claimants to their lineage have created different misguidances under different names in the place of this divine guidance, and have started considering these very misguidances as the religion of their ancestors. Through this reminder of its, the Quran is reminding of both the forgotten pages of history and the forgotten lessons of nature. So, whoever wishes, let him benefit from it.

Section 113: Verses 91 to 94: It was mentioned above that the call to the Quran, Monotheism (Tawhid), and Islam is the shared call of all the Prophets. Those who are rejecting it are victims of their own deprivation and misfortune. Now, in the following verses, a commentary is provided on the attitude of these deniers, and by referencing their statements, their absurdity is clarified. Naturally, the chiefs of Mecca were at the forefront of these deniers, but from the very beginning, the Jews began to support them because they considered the Prophet's (peace be upon him) call a primary threat to themselves. They were entrenched in the belief that Prophethood and Messengership were the monopoly of their family; if a Prophet were to come at all, it would be among the Children of Israel, how could it come from outside this lineage? Faced with this situation, they were in a severe struggle as to how to stop this threat. It was impossible to remain neutral, but if they opposed it, in what manner should they do so? If they said that Prophethood is exclusive to their family and that with the presence of the Torah, there is no need for another Quran or Book, they feared that this would ignite the tribal pride of the Arabs, and it would not be surprising if, in that fervor, they rose up to support Muhammad (peace be upon him). Because of this apprehension, they kept their true thoughts hidden, but to encourage the opponents of the Prophet, they began to say that anyone who claims that God has sent down a book upon him is absolutely wrong; God has not sent down anything upon anyone. Although they said this with their specific mental reservation, due to the political expediency we mentioned, they presented it in the form of a general principle. The Quran here has unmasked this mischief of theirs and also answered those arrogant ones among the Quraish who, merely out of their pride of leadership, were not ready to accept that someone outside their circle could be granted any honor or dignity by God. In this light, recite the following verses.

And this is a Book We have sent down, blessed, confirming what was before it... The previous answer, as we mentioned, was based on the apparent aspect of their statement. Now, an answer is provided addressing the hidden thought in their minds: why did God Almighty send down this Book after the Torah? What deficiency was fulfilled by its descent? In the previous verse, it was implicitly indicated what those who claimed to believe in the Torah actually did with the Torah, but that was an incidental answer. Now, the independent status and independent necessity of the Quran have been clarified: this Book is not a book that has come to their knowledge for the first time. This is the blessed Book whose glad tidings were given to Abraham and whose coming was prophesied by Moses and Jesus, all of them. It is the manifestation of those Abrahamic glad tidings and the confirmation of those very prophecies that already existed. Here, the words 'blessed' (mubarak) and 'confirmer' (musaddiq) for the Quran represent the validity that exists in the previous scriptures. We have already quoted the references in the Tafseer of Surah Al-Baqarah and Surah Aal-e-Imran. The word 'blessed' points towards that universal blessing whose glad tidings were given to Prophet Abraham (peace be upon him) that it would manifest through the Final Prophet (peace be upon him) born from the lineage of Ishmael. In Genesis, Chapter 24, it says, 'By Myself I have sworn, declares the Lord, because you have done this and have not withheld your son, your only son, I will surely bless you and will surely multiply your offspring as the stars of heaven... and in your offspring shall all the nations of the earth be blessed, because you have obeyed my voice.'

And who is more unjust than one who invents a lie about Allah... After the mischief-makers among the People of the Book, attention was turned to the leaders of the polytheists of Mecca, and after briefly referencing the nonsense they spoke regarding the Quran, their attention was drawn to their ultimate fate. Since their statements were entirely meaningless and merely a demonstration of their pride and arrogance, instead of answering those statements, the main blow was struck at their pride and arrogance. Their mention was initiated with the mention of their greatest injustice, which is polytheism. We have clarified elsewhere regarding polytheism that by committing it, man violates the greatest right of his Lord and also insults his own self the most. Therefore, this is the greatest injustice. Furthermore, it is a false fabrication against God because a polytheist merely designates something as a partner to God from his own mind and claims that God has declared it His partner, whereas he has no evidence for this claim.

And you have certainly come to Us alone... Here, the speaker has shifted from the third person to the second person style so that the horror of the evil end referred to in 'If you could see' comes before the eyes of the imagination, as if the Day of Judgment has arrived and they are being addressed, saying, 'Look, just as you came into the world without any possessions, today you have come before Us in the same state, without anything.' Neither is the wealth and might with you upon which you used to boast, nor are those partners and intercessors whom you believed to be our associates and from whom you expected that they would be of use to you in front of Us. 'We created you the first time' points to the fact that when man comes into the world, he is a lump of flesh and practically devoid of all abilities, then God Almighty nurtures all his abilities and makes him the owner of those resources, means, wealth, and property that are destined for him, in order to see whether, after receiving these blessings of God, he becomes a grateful servant or becomes arrogant and boastful. Then one day comes when he is presented before God empty-handed in the same way.

Indeed, Allah is the cleaver of grain and date seeds... Every single atom is a witness to Monotheism (Tawhid). 'Habb' (grain) and 'Nawa' (date seed) refer to the seed and the stone. First, the statement of God's signs began with small things, then attention was drawn to the great things of the universe. It is said that no one except God has the power to split even a tiny seed or a small stone and bring forth sprouts from them, and then make them plants and trees. It is God's power and His wisdom that He deposits potential within every seed and every stone, then to bring those potentials to fruition, He commands the earth, the sky, the clouds, the wind, the heat, and the cold, autumn and spring, all to play their part in its growth. It is as if every grain growing in this earth is testifying with its existence that it came into being through the control of the One whose control encompasses this entire universe.

He is the cleaver of the morning... Meaning the same God whose attributes are thus manifested within the seed and stone buried in the earth, just raise your eyes and see His attributes in the sky as well. Just as He splits the stone to produce a tree from within it, in the same way, He tears the veil of night to make the morning emerge from within it. Then it is said that He made the night for your rest and to remove your weariness; you remove the fatigue of the day in it, and your powers and nerves are refreshed in it to descend into the field of action anew. From this, it automatically emerges that He brings the morning after the night so that you can occupy yourselves in the activities of your search and quest and strive for your livelihood.

And it is He who placed for you the stars... After the sun and moon, attention was turned to the stars, saying, 'Look, they themselves are testifying through their service that the Creator has appointed them for your service.' When you travel on land and sea, they, like lighthouses and towers, show your ships and your caravans directions and paths. Now, how stupid it is that they themselves are showing by their actions that your Creator has harnessed them for your service, and you, considering them partners in God's divinity, set up their idols and begin their worship! Then look, they are in the sky, but they are performing the service of candle-bearers for you, the dwellers of the earth. Think, if the god of the earth were different and the gods of the heavens were different, what was it to the stars of the sky that they would ruin their sleep by standing awake all night to create paths for the dwellers of the earth?

And it is He who produced you from one soul... After drawing attention to the signs of the external world, now attention is drawn to man's own creation and the means of livelihood and economy He has provided for him in his surroundings. It is said that He is the same God who created you from a single soul and settled a vast family of humanity. Here, the word 'insha' (creation/nurture) is used, which does not only mean to create, but also contains the concept of nurturing, raising, and fostering. 'One soul' refers to Adam, who is accepted as the origin of humanity in all heavenly religions. The Arabs also accepted this truth. It is said that it is God who created you from a single soul, created its mate from its gender, and then created a world of men and women. Although there is a difference in their shapes, forms, languages, and accents, there is agreement regarding innate needs and natural inclinations, which is proof that the Creator and Lord of all is one who created all this foliage from a single tree.

And it is He who sends down rain from the sky... Now attention is being drawn to those effects and proofs of God's mercy, lordship, power, wisdom, monotheism, and resurrection which every human can see at every step, provided he wants to believe. We have written elsewhere about the word 'sama' (sky) that it comes for clouds as well, and also for that azure space which we call the sky. It is said that He is the same God who sends down rain from the sky and gives life to all things through it. The speech began with the third person, then changed to the first person 'fa-akhrajna' (We brought forth). In this, there is an expression of tenderness, favor, and lordship, and also an indication of the truth that We have dominion over the sky, earth, clouds, and wind. If someone else had dominion over the sky and someone else over the earth, then where would this compatibility manifest that rain would fall from the sky and the earth would bring forth its treasures?

But they have attributed to Allah partners—the jinn, while He created them... Meaning every single thing in the universe is providing testimonies related to God and His attributes, which were mentioned above, but the state of the foolishness and stupidity of these people is such that they have made the jinn partners to God as well. The Arabs included angels, jinn, and stars among the things they considered partners to God. But here, by mentioning the jinn first, the Quran has clarified the extreme vileness of polytheism, that where is that status of God which was described, and where is this folly of these absurd people, that even a ghost under a pipal tree and the devil have been made partners to God!

Originator of the heavens and the earth... 'Badi' (Originator) means bringing into existence from non-existence, meaning God is the One who brought the heavens and the earth and the entire universe into existence from non-existence. When there was nothing, God was there; this is an accepted truth. This means that since everything in this universe is God's creation, then how did any creation attain the status of sons and daughters, and through what path did they become partners in God's divinity? How can He have a child when He has no mate? Meaning, claiming a child for God opens the way for an even greater stupidity. That is, (God forbid) even a wife is claimed for God.

That is Allah, your Lord; there is no deity except Him... It is said that the same God who created you is also your Lord. Then what is the sense in considering Him the Creator but making others your Lord? Since He is the only Creator, worship Him alone, and since He is watching over everything, make Him the only source of your hope and fear.

Vision perceives Him not, but He perceives all vision... Meaning if you think that God is not visible, then this is not something to be disappointed about. Your eyes are indeed incapable of grasping Him, but He grasps your eyes. He who wants to see Him, although he cannot see Him, He sees the seeker. In the Hadith, it is said: 'Worship your Lord as if you are seeing Him; if you are not seeing Him, then He is surely seeing you.' The prohibition of carving physical idols of God: It should be noted here that one of the important factors in polytheism and idol worship has been that the ignorant wanted to see God in some physical form. This is what gave birth to intermediaries and means between man and God. When God was not seen anywhere by the eyes, ignorant people started worshiping idols carved in the image of those things they considered manifestations of God or His incarnations.

There has come to you enlightenment from your Lord... The word 'basirah' (enlightenment) is used in the Quran to mean understanding and also to mean the arguments and proofs that produce understanding. Here, it is in this second meaning, and it refers to the Wise Quran and its verses, which remove the veils from the eyes, provided someone wants to open their eyes. So whoever sees, it is for his own soul, and whoever is blind, it is against it; and I am not a guardian over you. The last words of the verse, 'and I am not a guardian over you,' are proof that the revelation of this verse is directly upon the tongue of Prophethood. It was not said, 'Tell them,' but rather the Prophet himself directly said the statement.

And thus do We diversify the verses... The explanation of 'tasreef' (diversification) of the verses has occurred in various places, meaning to clarify God's signs with various aspects and diverse styles. Here, 'thus' refers to the same arguments of Monotheism, Resurrection, and Messengership which were described in detail above. The Quran provides verses in diverse styles and aspects so that if they want to understand, they may understand, and if they want to persist in their ways, at least they can acknowledge that you have read and recited them thoroughly, and also because We are clarifying them well so that those who are seekers of knowledge may acquire knowledge from them.

106 to 167: This is an address to the Prophet. It means that you remain firm on Divine revelation and your rightful position. There is no deity but Allah, and do not care about the opposition of these polytheists. Turn away from them and remember that if God had liked compulsion in the matter of His religion, none of them could have remained on polytheism. He would have led everyone to the straight path of Monotheism and Islam, but His wisdom demanded that He test people by giving them choice in this matter: who chooses the path of Monotheism and who chooses polytheism? So when Divine wisdom wanted this, why are you worried about their matter? Your responsibility is to deliver the truth clearly, and you are performing this duty.

And do not insult those they invoke other than Allah, lest they insult Allah in enmity without knowledge... This is a timely guidance for the Muslims, just as guidance passed in Surah An-Nisa, verses 86 and 148. Just as the attitude of the hypocrites was criticized severely in the aforementioned verses, Muslims were also forbidden from cutting off relations with them and insulting them by naming individuals, lest this matter cross the boundaries of principles and enter the realm of personalities. Similarly, the severe criticism of polytheism and polytheists here demanded that Muslims be guided not to adopt a tone that would lead the more enthusiastic Muslims to start speaking harshly about those things which these polytheists worship. This guidance was necessary because this era, as is clear from the verses, was one of heated debate, and in the heat of debate, respect for boundaries is generally not observed, whereas it is mandatory for Muslims to not exceed the boundaries even in dealing with the enemy.

And they swear by Allah their strongest oaths that if there came to them a sign, they would surely believe in it... 'Jahd' means to try to the extent of one's ability, full exertion. The propaganda of the infidels against the Prophet (peace be upon him): 'They swear by Allah their strongest oaths' means that they swear oaths to convince the Prophet and the Muslims that if any miracle is shown according to their demand, they will surely believe that this miracle is from God, its demonstrator is God's Prophet, and the presented book is God's Book. Obviously, the intention behind convincing with oaths was not what they appeared to show, but it was a propaganda against the Prophet. On one hand, they wanted to satisfy those among their people who were being influenced by the Prophet's truthfulness that they had set a very reasonable condition, and if Muhammad (peace be upon him) fulfills this condition, they are ready to believe. On the other hand, they wanted to impress upon the hearts of the sincere Muslims that if these are true Messengers, then what is the barrier to accepting this condition?

And We will turn away their hearts and their eyes just as they refused to believe in it the first time... This is a statement of that Divine law under which some are granted faith and some are deprived of it. The explanation of this Divine law has occurred in various places in this Book. In this universe and within man's own existence, the Creator of the universe has spread countless of His signs. Those who reflect upon them and hold fast to the self-evident results that come before them from this reflection, are granted the bounty of faith. Conversely, those who, despite seeing all these signs, remain blind and deaf and remain engrossed in their self-worship, and do not open their eyes even after the Prophet's repeated reminders, God turns their hearts and their eyes away, the result of which is that they lose the ability for true thought and perception.

And even if We had sent down to them the angels [with the message] and the dead spoke to them and We gathered together every [created] thing in front of them, they would not believe... The main veil to faith: This is the emphasis on the same theme as above. It is said that even if We sent down angels upon them, as they say, or if the dead emerged from the graves and began talking to them, as they demand, or if We brought all the things of the unseen before them group by group, even then they would not believe because that tyranny which has bandaged their eyes would still remain. 'Except that Allah should will, but most of them are ignorant.' The form of their believing is only if Allah wills to grant them faith and guidance, and Allah's willing is not against the wisdom He has set and liked.

And thus We have made for every prophet an enemy—devils of mankind and jinn... 'Thus' refers to the opposition and turmoil that the leaders of the Quraish and their associates had raised in this era to harass the Prophet (peace be upon him). It is said, do not think that you alone have faced this situation; the prophets who passed before you also had to face such suffering at the hands of the devils of jinn and mankind of their own eras. This is the Divine law of testing through which all His prophets and messengers had to pass; it is through this that the truthfulness of the truthful is tested, and their essence is polished, and it is through this that the people of falsehood get the respite in which their inner corruption is manifested and they complete their proof against themselves.

And [it is] so the hearts of those who disbelieve in the Hereafter will incline toward it... The attribution of this verse is to the meaning that emerges from the previous sentence, meaning the respite We have given in this world to the devils of jinn and mankind to oppose the prophets and the righteous and to inspire innovations and superstitions, We have given so that on one hand, the truthfulness of the truth-seekers is tested, and on the other hand, the people of falsehood get respite, and finding their desired food at the hands of these devils and mischief-makers, they are attracted towards it, like it, and do the earning they are supposed to do in this world. This respite is the necessary result of the choice granted to man; therefore, it is under the Divine law. Those people's characteristic here is described as 'those who do not believe in the Hereafter,' which clarifies that this invitation of devils and mischief-makers only appeals to those who are empty of the belief in the Hereafter; for them, only this world and its luxury are desirable, and they get its validity at the hands of these devils.

Then is it other than Allah I should seek as judge while it is He who has revealed to you the Book detailed? This is the answer from the Prophet to the debate of the polytheists that you are debating with me about polytheism, monotheism, and the lawful and prohibited. The question is, in this dispute—whether God has partners in His divinity or He is the sole ruler, what things He has made prohibited and what allowed—who has the right to become the judge? God alone, or someone else? If God alone has this right, and undoubtedly He alone has it, then how is it permissible for me to consider someone else a judge in this matter, while He has also sent down a Book to you to settle this dispute, in which He has decided every matter in detail? Now, on one side is this detailed Divine Book, and on the other side are your baseless innovations; whose word is worthy of being accepted?

And the word of your Lord has been fulfilled in truth and in justice. None can alter His words, and He is the Hearing, the Knowing. This points to the fact that today you find all these devils of jinn and mankind united in opposition to you and your call—this is not a matter for surprise; this is exactly the situation described in the story of Satan and Adam. Satan asked for respite to misguide the children of Adam, God gave him respite, Satan threatened that I will cause the majority of the descendants of Adam to fall into polytheism and idol worship, God said that if you do this, I will fill Hell with you and all your followers. The explanation of this theme passed under the Tafseer of Surah Al-Baqarah, verse 168. Here, the purpose of referencing it is that these people, falling into Satan's trap, have fulfilled his prophecy regarding themselves, and the decision God gave to Satan regarding such people has been fulfilled, and this decision is correct according to both the criteria of truth and justice.

And if you obey most of those upon the earth, they will mislead you from the way of Allah... The truth pointed to in verse 115 is explained here, and although the address is in the singular, the meaning is general. It means that the noise raised in this era against the call of Monotheism and in support of polytheistic innovations, do not care about it at all. This is not based on any evidence, authority, or any knowledge and argument, but is based entirely on following conjecture. These are merely wild guesses. 'They follow nothing but conjecture, and they are merely guessing.' This claim that the path they are following is the one told by God and is the legacy of Abraham is merely their fabrication. It has no connection with God and the Abrahamic nation.

Indeed, your Lord is most knowing of who strays from His way, and He is most knowing of the [rightly] guided. This address is also general. It means that do not fall into any misunderstanding by seeing this crowd; God knows who are straying from His way and who are rightly guided. So follow the path of those whom God is calling rightly guided, and avoid the ways of those whom God is declaring misguided. This is a warning for those who are on the path of misguidance along with glad tidings for the rightly guided group, because when Allah is well aware of both these groups, He will treat them according to His knowledge. The claims of those who are misguided but claim to be standard-bearers of guidance will be of no use.

Command to eat the cattle considered prohibited under polytheistic superstitions: We have pointed out above that when Islam declared all those cattle lawful and good which the polytheists had declared prohibited under their polytheistic superstitions, they raised a great hue and cry, saying, 'Look, this person has made things lawful for his followers that were prohibited since the time of our elders, Abraham and Ishmael.' In such matters, as we have indicated, the general mind is very sensitive and superstitious, so it easily accepts the effect of opposing propaganda. The Quran here, to remove this effect, said that only those things are prohibited which God has mentioned in detail in His Book; so do not care about these polytheistic prohibitions and permissions. The right to prohibit and permit belongs to God alone. If this right is acknowledged for anyone else, then this is making another a partner in God's rights, and this is polytheism.

And why should you not eat of that upon which the name of Allah has been mentioned, while He has explained in detail to you what He has forbidden you, excepting that to which you are compelled... The point is that when it has been clarified by God what things are prohibited, then where is there any room for hesitation in this matter for those who claim faith in God? 'In detail' here also points to the detail that has passed in the previous Surahs, for example, Surah An-Nahl, verses 114 to 116, and also to the detail that has been described in this Surah itself. Although the subject here is considering something prohibited under polytheistic superstitions and beliefs, not merely avoiding something on aesthetic grounds. But with this emphasis, the reason for stressing the eating of lawful things is that the matter of polytheism and Monotheism is so important in religion that Shariah does not tolerate even the slightest ambiguity in this regard.

And leave what is apparent of sin and what is concealed thereof... This is an emphasis on the same point from another subtle and precise aspect. Command to abandon both polytheism and manifestations of polytheism: Every evil has two aspects. One is its internal aspect, the other is its external aspect. This point can also be said in other words: one is its reality, whose dwelling is man's self and heart, and the other is those manifestations and forms in which it appears within human life. For example, polytheism has a reality, which is considering someone a partner in God's being, attributes, or rights; and it has manifestations and forms, for example, idols, altars, arrows of divination, Bahirah, Sa'ibah, Wasilah, Ham, and other things of this kind that are the practical manifestation and sign of some polytheistic belief or concept.

Prohibition of those lawful things upon which Allah's name was not taken: In the previous verse, the command was given to eat the things prohibited under polytheistic beliefs when Allah's name has been taken over them. In this verse, the prohibition of those lawful things upon which Allah's name was not taken—according to the belief of the polytheists—is declared. The polytheists raised a hue and cry over this prohibition just like the previous permission, because among them, it was not necessary for the lawfulness and purity of an animal sacrifice that Allah's name must be taken over it. The animals they considered lawful and good, the announcement of their prohibition from the Muslims' side must have hurt their religious pride, and they must have started this propaganda to incite the sentiments of their common people against the Muslims, saying, 'Look, these new religionists call us and all our forefathers forbidden-eaters, because according to them, that animal is not allowed upon which Allah's name was not taken.'

And is one who was dead... Here, 'death' refers to the life of disbelief, and 'life' refers to the life of faith. 'Light' refers to the Book mentioned in verses 114 and 119, which God Almighty sent down for distinction between truth and falsehood and for the detail of the lawful and prohibited; 'darkness' refers to those conjectures and superstitions and those desires and innovations which were indicated in verses 116 and 119. Example of the people of faith and the people of disbelief: This is an example of the people of faith and these polytheists, that God has granted the people of faith the life of faith after the death of disbelief and has granted them a clear light in the form of His Book, from which they themselves are getting light and are showing the path to others.

And thus We have placed within every city the greatest of its criminals to conspire therein... 'Ja'alna' (We have placed) here is in the meaning of 'amhalna' (We have given respite), and 'thus' refers to the situation mentioned above in verse 121. This is the statement of the same Divine law in a slightly different style which was described above in verse 112. The point is that the efforts these devils of jinn and mankind are making to oppose this call is not a rare event. When the call of truth rises in any city, those who are standard-bearers of falsehood and whose interests are associated with this falsehood rise up with all their ploys to suppress it, and God Almighty, according to His law, gives them respite for a specific time so that the earning they want to do, they may do.

And when a sign comes to them, they say, 'Never will we believe until we are given like that which was given to the messengers of Allah.' This is an example of the ploy mentioned above. It means that when a sign came to them, whether it was a miracle or some guidance and warning, they would say that we are not ready to believe until we are also given the Messengership which these messenger people claim, after all, what special feathers did they have that God made them His messenger and ignored us, whereas leadership and sovereignty, and wealth and authority have been our share for generations? Exactly this, as described in detail in the Quran, the leaders of the Quraish used to say.

So whoever Allah wants to guide—He expands his breast to [contain] Islam... 'Haraj' refers to a narrow place filled with bushes. Here it has come as further emphasis after 'dayyiq' (narrow), 'tas'ad' is known for climbing a height with difficulty and exertion. 'Sama' (sky), just as it comes for the sky and clouds, similarly it also comes for space and its height, for example, 'A good word is like a good tree, its root is firm and its branches are in the sky.' Main reason for fleeing from faith: Now the main reason for their not believing is being described: it is that they have become deprived of Allah's help and guidance. It means all this talk and all these ploys are merely a veil of appearance.

And this is the path of your Lord, [leading] straight... Since the meaning of a verb is found within the demonstrative pronoun, 'mustaqiman' (straight) is a state (hal) from 'sirat' (path). The point is that this Islam is the straight path opened by God, the path of nature's Monotheism, it has no twists and turns, no climbs and gorges, but those who have loaded so many unnatural impurities upon themselves find this path arduous, 'We have explained the signs in detail for a people who remember.' That is, We have described all the arguments for it being the religion of nature and the nation of Abraham in detail, but arguments are useful for those who have the intention to remember and think and understand.

For them will be the Home of Peace with their Lord... This is the reward described for those whose breasts Allah expands for Islam. It is said that in return for this Islam, they will have the 'Home of Peace,' a home of comfort and peace with their Lord, and Allah will be their companion, whereas for the opponents of Islam, there will be a home of humiliation and punishment, and their companions will be those devils of jinn and mankind whose satanic inspiration they accepted, which led them astray. This piece is exactly opposite to the piece 'so those who committed crimes will suffer.'

And [mention] the Day when He will gather them together... 'Hum' (them) refers to those leaders of criminals mentioned in verses 123 to 124, and the emphasis of 'jami'an' (all) shows that along with the devils of mankind, those devils of jinn will also be gathered whose inspirational promptings they followed, as their mention has been coming since verse 112 above. Allah Almighty, on the Day of Judgment, gathering them all, will address the devils of jinn, saying, 'O company of jinn, you have misappropriated many of mankind.' Obviously, 'company of jinn' here does not refer to the entire nation of jinn, but rather the same group among them which followed their guide and leader Iblis, who himself, as proved by the Quran, was among the jinn and who deceived Adam and refused to obey the command of prostration and claimed with great pride, 'Do You see this one whom You have honored above me? If You respite me until the Day of Resurrection, I will surely destroy his descendants, except for a few.'

And thus do We make some of the wrongdoers allies of others... This points to the fact mentioned above in the piece 'O company of jinn, you have misappropriated many of mankind,' and 'wali' (ally/master), 'tawliyat fulanan al-amr' means to make someone the ruler, guardian, and possessor over something and to impose them over it. The point is that these jinn devils gained control over many humans; the reason for this is not that these devils were granted authority over humans, but the cause was their own deeds. They left God's guidance and followed their desires and innovations. The result was that they became followers of the devil, and Allah's rule is that He imposes devils over those who become followers of devils. 'Wrongdoers' here refers to those who choose the path of disbelief and polytheism.

O company of jinn and mankind, did there not come to you messengers from among you, relating to you My verses and warning you of the meeting of this Day of yours? This question will be asked of them to remove any excuse. The point is, O wretched ones, did messengers not come to you from among yourselves, reciting My verses and warning you of the coming of this day? If messengers had not come, you could have made some excuse; now what excuse can you make? You heard and understood everything and kept your eyes and ears closed. 'Messengers from among you' clearly informs that just as Allah Almighty sent messengers from among humans to humans, He sent messengers from among jinn to jinn. The Divine law regarding prophets and messengers, which is described in detail in the Quran, its necessary requirement is that messengers come from among the jinn to the jinn, who, in their language and according to their needs and circumstances, complete the proof of God upon them.

That is because your Lord would not destroy the cities for wrongdoing while their people were unaware. This is the reason stated for why such arrangements were made that in every nation, whether humans or jinn, messengers were sent from among them. It is said that this happened because it was far from your Lord's mercy that He should send punishment upon any nation for its disbelief and polytheism without informing them of its results and consequences. If He punishes anyone, He informs them thoroughly beforehand so that if they want to repent and reform, they may repent and reform, and if they do not want to, they may face the consequences on their own responsibility.

And for all are degrees from what they have done... We have written in various places about the word 'kull' (all), that when it comes like this, it refers to those people or parties mentioned above. Here, it refers to the same groups of jinn and humans mentioned above. It is said that they did whatever they did knowingly, after the completion of proof from God; therefore, it is right that they should suffer the punishment of their deeds, and those who have degrees of crimes, according to that, they should find their place in Hell. Since Allah is well aware of their deeds which they have been doing or are doing, therefore He will not face any difficulty in their classification. He will deal with each one according to what he deserves for his deeds.

And your Lord is the Free of need, the possessor of mercy... It is said that your Lord is also free of need (Ghani) and the possessor of mercy (Dhu-Rahmah). We have clarified elsewhere that when attributes are mentioned without a conjunction like this, it means that these attributes are present in the described (the Lord) at the same time. He is at once both Ghani and merciful. Due to His attribute of self-sufficiency, He does not care about anyone. He is the most independent, the most self-sufficient, and the most indifferent of all. If everyone denies Him, it does not harm Him in the slightest. If everyone sings songs of His praise, it does not add anything to Him. If He sends His messengers, sends down a Book, and invites to faith and Islam, it is not because His work is stuck without people's faith and Islam, but rather He does all this because, besides being independent, He is also merciful.

Indeed, what you are promised is coming, and you will not cause failure [to Allah]. 'Tu'adun' includes the punishment of which they were warned in the event of rejecting the Messenger, and also the Day of Judgment which is an inevitable truth of this universe. It is said that both these things are inevitable, and when they manifest, you will not be able to escape God's control in any way. 'A'jaza al-shay'—fatahu wa lam yaqdir 'alayhi' (it escaped his control and he could not grasp it). This warning implies that do not hasten for punishment; if you do not change your ways, this thing is bound to come.

Say, 'O my people, work according to your position; indeed, I am working... In accordance with the known style of Arabic, there is a counterpart omitted here. Meaning, I am working according to my position. 'Makanah' (position) means place, status, and position; in the Quran, it is used in the meaning of place. The concept of methodology is among the requirements of this word. When we say work according to your place, I work according to my place, the concept of methodology is automatically born within it. It means that since you are not ready to move from your position of disbelief and stance of opposing the truth, then do what you want to do. I am also not ready to move an inch from my position. Those who know the taste of Arabic know that in 'I am working,' more emphasis is placed than in 'work' (imperative), because the forms of adjective and subject manifest permanence, continuity, independence, and determination compared to the verb.

And they assigned to Allah from that which He created of crops and livestock a share and said, 'This is for Allah,' by their claim, 'and this is for our partners [associated with Him].' Meaning, God created fields, crops, and cattle, but these unjust people, out of what they took as God's share, distributed it in such a way that they separated a share for God along with other partners. 'This is for Allah,' by their claim, 'and this is for our partners'—this is the detail of their distribution, that this much share is Allah's and this much is for our partners and deities. Regarding this distribution, it is said that 'by their claim'—all this is based on their conjecture, but the claim is that this is the command of the Abrahamic Shariah.

And likewise, to many of the polytheists, their partners have made [seem] pleasing the killing of their children in order to bring about their destruction and to cover them with confusion in their religion... Meaning these partners of theirs are not only partners in their wealth, but they have also been made partners in the lives granted by God. Many polytheists, to please them, even sacrifice their children for them. We have indicated under verse 100 that this grievous crime was committed to please some assumed tyrannical jinn/demons. Perhaps the hard-hearted ignorant polytheists, under the superstition that if they did not please the jinn of a certain valley or a certain tree by sacrificing one of their children, he would bring disaster upon all their children or all their assets, used to do this act.

And they say, 'These animals and crops are forbidden; no one may eat from them except whom we will,' by their claim... 'Hijr' means prohibited, but this is a religious term of the pagan Arabs. It refers to that thing which is prohibited under some religious ritual—the Islamic term for this is 'haram' (forbidden). These are the fabricated edicts of the priests and caretakers of their shrines and idols being quoted. Among them, regarding the eating of offerings presented of crops and cattle, there were great restrictions and limitations based on the nature of the offering. For example, men can eat, women cannot, or a widow can eat, but a newlywed or virgin cannot touch it; this kind of stupidity is also found among superstitious families in our society.

And they say, 'What is in the bellies of these animals is exclusively for our males and forbidden to our wives. But if it is born dead, then they all share therein.' This is also a statement of the same kind of self-made edicts. Regarding the young born from the bellies of these cattle, their edict under polytheistic superstitions was that it is lawful for men to eat them, but forbidden for women. Yes, if the young is born dead, then men and women can both share in eating it. It is said, 'He will compensate them for their description; indeed, He is Wise, Knowing.' The self-made edicts being issued, Allah will soon compensate them for this. It is against His knowledge and wisdom that He should not punish those who open factories of Shariah-making with this audacity in His name.

Those will have lost who killed their children in foolishness without knowledge and prohibited what Allah had provided for them, fabricating lies about Allah... This is an expression of regret over all these stupidities mentioned above and the evil end of their perpetrators—that without any divine authority, merely out of stupidity, by fabricating lies about Allah, they killed their children and prohibited upon themselves the provision God had given them. What room is there for doubt in their misfortune and failure? They strayed from the path of truth, and God showed them His path through His Prophet, but due to their misfortune, they did not become the ones to accept it. 'They have strayed and were not [rightly] guided.'

And He it is who causes gardens to grow, [both] trellised and untrellised... Meaning fields and gardens were all created by God, so on what basis have you made other goddesses and gods partners in them? 'Ma'rushat' refers to grapes, etc., whose vines are trained on trellises. 'Ghayra ma'rushat' refers to those things which do not need trellises. Witness of things in the universe through the language of state: One is that the One who created all this is very generous and kind, benevolent, and provider, generous and kind to servants. He placed the need for provision in servants, but He did not just create the means to fill the stomach anyhow, but rather placed heaps of varieties of blessings. When He grew gardens, He grew them in diverse types; when He created dates and crops, He created countless types; when He granted olives, pomegranates, and other fruits and flowers, He gave them in brand-new varieties.

And of the grazing livestock, He produces both beasts of burden and those for food... 'Hamulah' (beast of burden) is on the weight of 'rakubah' (ride) and 'halubah' (milker), and it refers to those cattle which are suitable for riding and carrying loads. For example, camels, horses, mules, etc. 'Farsh' is also called the land where vegetation is abundant. It also refers to the crop that has not yet stood on its stalks, and when this word comes in relation to animals, it refers to small animals which are not suitable for riding or carrying loads; they are suitable only for slaughter or other needs, for example, goats, sheep, etc. If the context is evidence, then small camels and cows are also included in it. But here this word is used in opposition to 'hamulah,' therefore necessarily small cattle are meant here.

Eight mates—two of the sheep and two of the goats... The eight are the object of the verb omitted. Generally, people have assumed an omitted past verb, but according to us, the evidence here is for assuming an omitted imperative verb. The explanation of the sentence will clarify this evidence. The word 'zawj' (mate/pair) comes for a pair, and it also comes for one individual of a pair. There are precedents for this in Arabic literature and the Quran both. Application of the word 'An'am' (livestock) to cattle: Among domestic cattle that were found in Arabia for meat or milk, etc., four were known. Among small cattle: sheep, goat; among large cattle: camel, cow. The word 'an'am' is spoken for these same ones. When the word 'bahimah' is related to it, then as we have indicated in the beginning of Surah Al-Ma'idah, wild cattle which are not domestic but are included in the genus of 'an'am' are also included in it.

Or were you witnesses when Allah charged you with this? This is the final step of the completion of proof. The point is that as far as arguments of reason and transmission are concerned, none of these things is with you; then on what basis are you fabricating this slander against Allah that God has prohibited such-and-such things? Were you present when God commanded these things? Then tell us, who can be more deprived of blessings, miserable, and unjust than those who, without any academic authority or rational argument, merely use a fabricated lie as a means to mislead people? 'Indeed, Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people' refers to the same people who were pointed to above in 'Who is more unjust?' The point is that those who are such unjust people that they are not only using fabricated slander as an excuse for their misguidance but are also making it a means to mislead others, Allah will not guide such people.

Say, 'I do not find within that which was revealed to me [anything] forbidden to one who would eat it unless it be a dead animal or blood spilled out or the flesh of swine—for indeed, it is impure—or it be [that slaughtered in] disobedience, dedicated to other than Allah.' Now, the announcement has been made through the tongue of the Prophet (peace be upon him) that what things among the cattle were prohibited in the Abrahamic nation. From the previous debates, it is clear that the debate going on here with the pagan Arabs is related to the Abrahamic nation itself, and this debate is especially related to cattle because they had prohibited some of them under their polytheistic superstitions, as is clear from the statement above. The specificity of this occasion and place requires that this announcement be viewed from this specific angle.

And to those who were Jews, We prohibited every animal of uncloven hoof... Reason for some good things being prohibited to the Jews: 'And to those who were Jews, We prohibited every animal of uncloven hoof, and of the cattle and the sheep We prohibited to them their fat.' The Abrahamic nation was mentioned; now the nation of the Jews is mentioned, that some good things were indeed prohibited for them. For example, all animals with claws and the fat of cows and goats, etc. But this prohibition was not because these things were worthy of prohibition in themselves, but they were prohibited to them as a punishment for their rebellion. The point is that now this prohibition does not remain in the Islamic nation; rather, all good things have been made lawful in it, and the 'weights and chains' which were imposed upon the Jews, as was promised to them, were removed after the coming of the Final Prophet.

So if they deny you, [O Muhammad], say, 'Your Lord is possessor of vast mercy...' Now this is a warning to both the Quraish and the Israelites. Addressing the Prophet, it is said that after all this clarification, if they still do not believe in you, remain adamant in your denial, then tell them that since God possesses very vast mercy, He gives respite to the rebellious and the adamant for a long time and for a long distance so that no excuse remains for them; therefore, He is giving you respite after respite. Do not be proud of this respite; respite is respite after all. With this, the timing of God's seizing is delayed, but this does not invalidate His law which He has set for seizing the criminals. When the time for the manifestation of His law comes, it will not be averted by anyone's turning away.

The final opposition of the polytheists: 'Those who associated others [with Allah] will say, 'If Allah had willed, we would not have associated [anything] and neither would our fathers, nor would we have prohibited anything.'' Now, the final opposition of the polytheists is quoted, and it is answered. When the polytheists were defeated in debate from every side, they would say the final thing that if we have opposed God's will in the matter of associating partners to God and prohibiting something, then God has control over everything; why did He not stop us with His control? When He did not stop us with the force of His control, it means that whatever we did and are doing, this is His command and His will. Obviously, this opposition is a completely stupid opposition.

Say, 'With Allah is the far-reaching argument. If He had willed, He would have guided you all.' Divine law in the matter of guidance and misguidance: This is the original law of Allah Almighty in the matter of guidance and misguidance, which was clarified that Allah's way is not that He leads whomsoever He wills to guidance by the force of His will. If He wanted to do that, no one could have stopped this will of His. He would have guided you all, rather all creation, to guidance, but in this matter, He has not liked compulsion but leads through argument and evidence, and He has given people the choice that if they want, they may accept this guidance, and if they want, they may reject it.

Two foundations for testimony: Say, 'Bring forward your witnesses who will testify that Allah prohibited this.' The point is, do not run wild with conjectures. Yes, if you have witnesses who can testify that the things you have prohibited, God has prohibited them, then bring these witnesses forward. It should be noted that valid testimony is based on only two foundations: one on personal observation and personal knowledge/awareness; the second on some rational or natural argument, the example of which exists in Surah Yusuf, verses 26-28. Here, the Quran has demanded both these types of witnesses and testimonies that if there is any testimony of this kind, bring it forward, otherwise accept the 'far-reaching argument' which the Quran is presenting before you.

Detail of the original Abrahamic nation: Say, 'Come, I will recite what your Lord has prohibited to you. [He commands] that you not associate anything with Him...' This is a detailed statement of the original Abrahamic nation. It was commanded through the tongue of the Prophet (peace be upon him) that you have, merely from your conjecture, prohibited some good and lawful things of the Abrahamic nation under your polytheistic superstitions and have understood that this is all the Abrahamic nation and you have fulfilled its right, whereas you have adopted many other things in the Abrahamic nation that are prohibited regarding the rights and dealings of God and servants. So come, I will tell you what your Lord has prohibited upon you. It is said that the first thing prohibited upon you is polytheism, but you have made polytheism your religion.

And do not approach the property of an orphan except in the way that is best... The explanation of this has occurred in the Tafseer of Surah An-Nisa. Orphan's property is the fire of Hell; therefore, no one should approach it with bad intentions. Whoever goes to it, should go only in the best way, meaning to look after it and develop it as much as possible until the orphan reaches maturity. When he reaches maturity, hand over his property to him with full caution and in the presence of witnesses. 'And fulfill the measure and the weight with justice.' Measure and weight correctly and with justice, this is also a command given in the positive sense; therefore, its opposite aspect should also be kept in view, as we mentioned under 'And treat parents with goodness.'

And [moreover], this is My path, which is straight, so follow it; and do not follow [other] ways, for you will be separated from His way. 'Mustaqiman' (straight) is a state here from 'sirat' (path). We have already stated that the demonstrative pronoun contains the meaning of a verb, therefore it also does the work of a verb. Now, the final word of the sequence of speech has been issued that this straight path which I am presenting before you, just follow it. This is the path told by God, and this is the path told by Abraham, which he commanded his children to follow. Avoid the paths that have been carved out by moving away from this path. All those paths are far from this 'straight path' and are the ones that lead away from the Abrahamic nation.

Then We gave Moses the Book, completing [Our favor] upon him who did good and detailed explanation of all things and as guidance and mercy that they might believe in the meeting with their Lord. Connection between the Mosaic nation and the Abrahamic nation: 'Thumma' (then) shows order. Above, as is clear, was the statement of the Abrahamic nation. After Prophet Abraham (peace be upon him), the messenger who was a law-giver and book-giver is Prophet Moses (peace be upon him). It is said that after Abraham, We gave the Book to Moses so that We might complete Our bounty of Shariah upon His good servant, We gave in it the detail of every necessary matter, and made it guidance and mercy so that people might believe in meeting their Lord in the Hereafter.

And this is a Book We have revealed [which is] blessed, so follow it... After the Torah, the descent of the Quran is mentioned, and through it, the favor God Almighty bestowed upon all creation generally and the people of Arabia specifically is referenced first, then the Arabs were warned that through this Book, proof has been completed upon you. Now, no excuse remains for you in front of God; therefore, think of the consequences of rejecting this Book if you reject it.

[Or] that you say, 'The Book was only revealed to two groups before us, but we were of their study unaware.' 'An taqulu' (lest you say)—meaning, 'kara'atan an taqulu' (out of dislike that you might say). This style and this theme passed in Surah Al-Ma'idah, verse 19. The point is that Allah has sent down this mercy upon you so that you might not make an excuse tomorrow in front of God, saying, 'Just as the Book was granted to the Jews and Christians, why was the Book not granted to us in the same way? Their knowledge and study could be of no use to us; we remained unaware of their knowledge, study, and their teaching and learning.'

Or that you say, 'If only the Book had been revealed to us, we would have been better guided than they.' Or make the excuse that if the Book had been revealed to us, we would have been better at accepting guidance than them; then why were we deprived of this blessing! 'So there has come to you a clear evidence from your Lord and guidance and mercy,' meaning to finish all these excuses of yours, God has sent down a clear proof and guidance and mercy upon you. Now you cannot make any such excuse. The words 'clear evidence,' 'guidance,' and 'mercy' are used for the Quran.

Do they wait except that the angels should come to them, or your Lord should come, or there should come some of the signs of your Lord? The day some of the signs of your Lord will come, no soul will its belief benefit if it had not believed before or had earned through its belief some good. It means all their excuses are finished, and proof has been established upon them from every aspect, but these are not the ones to be convinced by arguments and proofs. They are waiting for angels to descend upon them, or for God to appear before them himself. If not this, then one of the decisive signs of God's punishment should manifest. The mention of the infidels' demands passed in Al-Baqarah 210 and Al-An'am 111. It will be mentioned in the following Surahs too.

Indeed, those who have divided their religion and become sects—you, [O Muhammad], are not [associated] with them in anything. Final guidance to the Prophet: Above in verse 153, it was said, 'And this is My path, which is straight, so follow it; and do not follow [other] ways, for you will be separated from His way.' We explained that this is the statement of the original Abrahamic nation, which was deposited with both branches of Abraham's lineage, the Israelites and the Ishmaelites, but both these branches created innovations in it and found various different ways. The Arabs chose the path of polytheism and idol worship, and the Jews and Christians set up the branches of Judaism and Christianity. In this way, the original highway was lost. Allah Almighty reopened this straight path for the world through the Final Prophet (peace be upon him), and as is clear from the previous debates of this Surah, explained its arguments in detail.

Whoever comes [on the Day of Judgment] with a good deed will have ten times the like thereof [to his credit], and whoever comes with an evil deed will not be recompensed except the like thereof; and they will not be wronged. This is the explanation of 'Then He will inform them of what they used to do.' The intention of informing them of their deeds, obviously, is not just to read them a report, but its necessity is reward and punishment. Thus, it has been clarified in this verse that whoever brings a good deed will get a tenfold reward, and whoever brings an evil deed will be punished precisely according to his evil. There will be no reduction for those who do good, nor any excess for those who do evil. It should be noted that the quantity of reward for good mentioned in the verse is the minimum.

Say, 'Indeed, my Lord has guided me to a straight path—a correct religion—the way of Abraham, inclining toward truth, and he was not of those who associated others with Allah.' 'Dinan qiyaman' (a correct religion) is a substitute (badal) for 'straight path,' and the occasion and place are to be considered. 'Qiyaman' and 'Qayyiman' are both synonymous, meaning a straight and natural religion in which there is no crookedness and deviation. In the Quran, this word is used for the Abrahamic nation and the Islamic nation, and the purpose is to clarify this aspect of this nation that it is completely clean from the deviation and deflection which the polytheists, Jews, and Christians created in their religion.

Say, 'Indeed, my prayer, my rites of sacrifice, my living and my dying are for Allah, Lord of the worlds.' Regarding the word 'nusuk' (rites of sacrifice), we have discussed it elsewhere; here 'nusuk' means sacrifice, and linking it with prayer provides evidence for this concept. In Surah Al-Kawthar, it is said, 'So pray to your Lord and sacrifice [to Him alone].' The original spirit of the Abrahamic nation and the Islamic nation: This is the expression of the original spirit of the Abrahamic nation and the Islamic nation through the tongue of the Prophet (peace be upon him). Prayer and sacrifice, living and dying—consider the very beautiful opposition. Living is in opposition to prayer, and dying is in opposition to sacrifice. This indicates that one who is on this nation, he lives for God, and he dies for God. There is no division in his life.

No partner has He. And this I have been commanded, and I am the first of the Muslims. God has no partner. Therefore, there is no partner in the servant's life either. It is completely, without any division and analysis and without any reservation and exception, only for Allah, the One, the Unique, who has no partner. It is said, 'And this I have been commanded, and I am the first of the Muslims,' this is my nature, and this is what I have been commanded from above. Because of this, I have moved forward and put this collar around my neck. The point is that I have started on this path; now whoever has the courage may accompany me on this path. I cannot postpone my journey waiting for others.

Say, 'Is it other than Allah I should desire as a lord while He is the Lord of all things?' This is the argument for the Islam and this surrender and devotion which was expressed in the above verse, that when Allah Almighty Himself is the Lord of everything, then how can I consider someone else a Lord and accept the partnership of someone else in my life? Every soul that earns [sin] will be responsible for it, no one else will become the carrier of its burden; to God is the return of all of you, and He will declare the decision of all those disputes in which you are engrossed today.

And it is He who has made you successors upon the earth and has raised some of you above others in degrees [of rank] that He may try you through what He has given you. Final warning to the Quraish: This is the final warning to the Quraish that you are not the first nation to appear on the stage of this world. How many nations appeared on this stage before you, then disappeared. To the extent that God made you their successor. This succession itself is proof that the Master of this world will weigh you with the same scale with which He weighed them, and if you do not come up to full measure in His scale, then just as He threw others away, He will also throw you away. The law of God that remained in the case of others, there is no reason for it to change in your case. This matter has been described briefly here. Its full detail is coming in the next Surah—Al-A'raf—which is its twin Surah.
